data.title: RAVE Liquidations Surge to $6.24 Million, Driven by Short Positions
data.description: RAVE liquidations have reached a total of $6.24 million in the past four hours. According to NS3.AI, this surge was primarily driven by $5.48 million in short liquidations, while long liquidations amounted to $760,000.

data.title: Trump Removes Controversial Social Media Post
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has taken down a social media post that portrayed him in a manner akin to Jesus Christ. Bloomberg posted on X that the image sparked criticism, even among his supporters, leading to its removal.

data.title: Crypto-Backed PAC Invests in Georgia Republican Ahead of Midterms
data.description: Fellowship, a super PAC aligned with cryptocurrency interests, has announced a $300,000 advertising campaign in support of Georgia Republican Clay Fuller as the U.S. midterm elections approach. According to NS3.AI, the PAC claims to have over $100 million from undisclosed donors. In addition to its support for Fuller, the group has endorsed Republican candidates in five other states. Jesse Spiro, Tether's head of government affairs, has been named as the chair of the PAC. This initiative coincides with the ongoing delay of the CLARITY Act in the Senate, which currently lacks a clear path forward.

data.title: Ukraine's Central Bank Aims for 5% Inflation Target in Three Years
data.description: Ukraine's central bank governor has announced that the National Bank of Ukraine is targeting an inflation rate of 5% within the next three years. According to Jin10, this goal is part of the bank's broader strategy to stabilize the country's economy and ensure sustainable growth. The central bank is implementing various monetary policies to achieve this target, aiming to create a stable economic environment conducive to investment and development. The governor emphasized the importance of maintaining a balanced approach to monetary policy to support economic recovery and growth.

data.title: Trump Indicates Potential Focus on Cuba Following Iran Resolution
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested that addressing Cuba could become a priority once the situation with Iran is resolved. According to NS3.AI, Trump's statement highlights a possible shift in focus, although it does not confirm any specific policy action regarding Cuba.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Expected to Speak Soon
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is anticipated to make a public statement shortly. According to NS3.AI, Fox News has reported on the potential timing of this address. Further details regarding the content or context of the speech have not been disclosed.

data.title: Trump: Things won't be pleasant if no deal is reached with Iran within two weeks.
data.description: US President Trump stated that the Strait of Hormuz blockade has begun, and other countries will provide assistance during the blockade, potentially resolving the Iranian issue before oil tankers reach the US. He added that the situation will not be pleasant if an agreement with Iran is not reached within two weeks. The timeline regarding Iran remains unchanged; there is currently no fighting, Iran has no business, and this situation will be maintained. Hopefully, there will be no need to release oil reserves again, but we are ready to take action at any time. (Jinshi)

data.title: RAVE Surges Over 250% in 24 Hours
data.description: RAVE has experienced a significant price increase, surpassing 11 USDT. According to Odaily, the current price is reported at 11.12569 USDT, marking a 24-hour rise of 253.83%.

data.title: Trump: 34 ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz yesterday.
data.description: US President Trump stated that 34 ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz yesterday, the highest number of ships since the strait was closed. He also announced that he will file an updated lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal on or before April 27th (local time). (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Reports Record Ship Passage Through Strait of Hormuz
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that 34 vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz yesterday, marking the highest traffic since its closure. According to Odaily, this record passage is significant amid ongoing tensions in the region. Trump also stated that an updated lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal will be filed by April 27, local time.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Seeks New Talks Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly planning to initiate a new round of negotiations with Iran before tensions escalate further. According to BlockBeats, Israeli media sources have indicated that Trump aims to address the conflict through diplomatic means.

Additionally, AXIOS reports that U.S. officials have confirmed ongoing contact between the United States and Iran, with efforts progressing towards reaching an agreement.

data.title: US media: The US demands Iran suspend uranium enrichment for 20 years; Iran hopes for a shorter suspension period.
data.description: According to AXIOS, a US official and a source familiar with the matter revealed that the US proposed a 20-year moratorium on uranium enrichment for Iran during negotiations in Islamabad over the weekend. Iran, however, proposed a shorter period, lasting only a few years. The source stated that the US demand for a moratorium on uranium enrichment was a key issue in these marathon negotiations. "The US proposed a moratorium of at least 20 years, with various other restrictions attached," said a source familiar with the matter. The US also demanded that Iran remove all highly enriched uranium from the country. Iran, on the other hand, indicated its willingness to accept a "monitored de-enrichment process" for this material. (Jinshi)

data.title: China Calls for Enhanced Peace Efforts Between Iran and the U.S.
data.description: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi has called on the international community to intensify efforts to facilitate peace talks between Iran and the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Wang's warning that the existing truce is fragile and requires preservation. The call for action underscores the delicate nature of the current situation and the need for sustained diplomatic engagement to maintain stability.

data.title: On April 13, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 3,353 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 29,225 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, on April 13, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 3,353 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 29,225 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 137,339 SOL.

data.title: GENIUS surged over 850% in a short period, pushing its market capitalization above $820 million.
data.description: According to GMGN market data, GENIUS's market capitalization briefly exceeded $820 million, and is currently trading at $716 million, representing a short-term increase of over 850%.

data.title: Iranian Defense Ministry spokesman: Any military attempt by Trump to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman will fail.
data.description: A spokesperson for the Iranian Ministry of Defense stated that any military attempt by US President Trump to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman will fail, and any foreign military intervention in the Strait of Hormuz will exacerbate the crisis and undermine global energy security. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Adobe and Tesco Forge AI Partnership to Enhance Customer Experience
data.description: Adobe has entered into a strategic partnership with Tesco to leverage artificial intelligence in creating personalized experiences for Tesco customers. According to Jin10, this collaboration aims to enhance customer loyalty rewards by utilizing AI-driven solutions. The partnership is expected to bring innovative approaches to customer engagement, focusing on tailored interactions and improved satisfaction. Both companies are committed to integrating advanced technologies to meet evolving consumer needs and preferences.

data.title: Kraken refused to compromise after being extorted: Approximately 2,000 accounts were affected, but funds remained safe.
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken has stated that it is being blackmailed by a criminal organization that has threatened to release videos of its internal system access. The company emphasized that no systemic intrusion occurred, customer funds remain safe, and it will not pay ransom to the attackers. Kraken disclosed that the incident stemmed from improper access by personnel within its customer service team, occurring in two separate incidents involving limited data access to approximately 2,000 accounts (about 0.02% of total users). The company has identified the individuals involved and terminated their access, while also notifying affected users and strengthening security controls. Kraken's Chief Security Officer, Nick Percoco, stated that the company is cooperating with law enforcement in the investigation and believes it has evidence to track down and arrest the individuals involved. He added that the current attacks exhibit a trend of "internal penetration + social engineering," reflecting the ongoing security challenges faced by the crypto industry in a high-value asset and complex technological environment. (CoinDesk)
